expectations.text: Engages with the plan's specific PocketBase rationale (pb_hooks for server logic, single binary / two deployable pieces, migrations as code) rather than a generic Supabase-vs-PocketBase feature comparison
expectations.evidence: Argues from pb_hooks/goja (no npm, hand-rolled Stripe verification, PDF-in-goja called out), pb_data backups, the two-deployable-pieces property, and section-by-section plan impact.

expectations.text: Acknowledges the user's timing point — that pre-Phase-0 is the cheapest moment to switch — rather than dismissing the question
expectations.evidence: "you're right that now is the cheapest it will ever be. The only artifact that exists is the plan document."

expectations.text: Presents at least two genuine alternatives / framings (e.g. switch now, stay, or a criteria-based tripwire for switching later)
expectations.evidence: Switch to Supabase; stay on PocketBase; and a third option: stay but relocate server logic to Next.js route handlers with an admin token.

expectations.text: Commits to a single clear recommendation rather than a neutral pros/cons list
expectations.evidence: "Short answer: yes, I'd switch" and a concrete closing directive (Supabase + route handlers, update plan sections before Phase 0).

expectations.text: Weighs the decision against solo part-time development reality (ops burden, hosting cost, learning curve) with concrete specifics
expectations.evidence: $25/mo Pro vs €4/mo Hetzner, free-tier pausing, RLS being easier to get subtly wrong, heavier local dev/CI, 'ops forever on the database that records who paid you.'

notes: Excellent baseline; the with-skill run's distinct additions were the zero-commit git check emphasis, the anti-churn motive test, and the decision-recording offer.
